bids.reasoning: At t=0.0 there is no need to concede materially, so I am targeting a near-maximum utility bid. The opponent’s opening offer gives me low utility on several important issues, but they selected issueD:valueB and issueE:valueA, which are acceptable to me; I can keep issueD:valueB at no cost while holding firm on my other high-value issues.

I am targeting a very high-utility bid because it is still extremely early at t=0.02, so there is little reason to make major concessions yet. The opponent improved from about 0.44 to 0.60 utility for me by moving issueA to valueA, but they are still holding low-value positions for me on issueB, issueC, and issueF. To avoid simply repeating my prior bid, I make a small concession on the lower-weight issueF while holding firm on the more important issues.

I am targeting a near-maximum utility bid because it is still very early at t=0.04, and the opponent’s offers have been improving for me from about 0.44 to 0.684 utility. They have already conceded on issueA and issueC while consistently offering issueD=valueB and issueE=valueA, so I keep those compatible values and hold firm on my important issueB and issueC preferences.

At t=0.06 it is still very early, so I am targeting a near-max utility bid while making only a small concession on the lower-weight issueF. The opponent has shown willingness to give me issueA or issueC in some offers, but they keep pushing issueB and issueF to valueC, so I am holding firm on the high-weight issueB and issueC while offering valueB on issueF as a modest tradeoff.

I am targeting a high utility bid around 0.92 because it is still very early, so there is no need to concede on high-weight issues like issueB. The opponent’s offers have improved for me overall, especially on issueA and issueC, but they remain firm on issueB=valueC and issueF=valueC, so I concede only the lower-weight issueF while holding my key preferences.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.964 because it is still very early, so there is little need to concede heavily yet. The opponent’s valid offers show they consistently want issueD=valueB and issueE=valueA, which are already acceptable to me, but they keep pushing issueB=valueC and issueF=valueC; I will only make a small concession on low-weight issueF while holding firm on high-weight issueB and issueC.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.90 because it is still early at t=0.14, so there is no need for major concessions yet.
